mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly reducing present equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p<0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where the trigger activates over a 5-year backtest, or the signal loses predictive power (p>0.1) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, boosting corporate revenue expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p>0.1) in a multivariate regression over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat during industrial production expansions within tightening cycles, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a rolling 4-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 198.0, elevated inflation expectations force markets to price in tighter financial conditions and margin compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold over a 10-year sample, or the Rank IC drops below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p>0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate profit growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no positive correlation with GDP growth, or the Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contractions in industrial production signal supply-side weakness and reduced business investment, which depresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive during industrial production contractions in late-cycle regimes, with a mean return exceeding 0.1% per month over a 5-year backtest.
